COD Slip Reference: SHP-2026-88821
Amount: MYR 1,200.00
Customer: Ahmad bin Razak
Delivery Address: No 12, Jalan Setia, Petaling Jaya
Agent: Khairul (ID: DHL-A-4421)
Customer called in to say the amount is wrong. Says original order was RM950 not RM1200. Very angry. Wants manager callback.

Complaint ID: EC-2026-55123
Tracking: MY-DHL-20263391
Consignee: Lim Wei Ling, Subang Jaya
Last scan: Out for Delivery
Customer note: No one came to my door. I was home all day.

[2026-04-29 11:23:41] ERROR 500 /api/track/bulk
SequelizeConnectionAcquireTimeoutError: Operation timeout
Pool size: 10/10 connections active
Query count: 847 in last 60s
Affected clients: Lazada, Shopee Enterprise, Zalora
Damaged carton detected. Compression marks visible on all sides. Fragile sticker present but partially torn. Laptop box deformed.

DHL Damage Report
Shipment: MY-DHL-20265544
Item: Laptop – ASUS VivoBook 15
Declared Value: MYR 3,200
Customer: Priya Nair, Mont Kiara

Shipment: MY-DHL-20261187
Consignee: Tan Mei Ling, Unit 12-A, Sri Petaling
POD Signature: Azman – not recognised by customer
Customer note: Package contains medical supplies urgently needed.

Client: AutoParts Sdn Bhd
Agreed Delivery: Apr 27 18:00
Actual Delivery: Apr 28 12:00
Delay: 18 hours

Receipt 1: RM250.00 – 10:23AM
Receipt 2: RM250.00 – 10:24AM
Same terminal: T-0041
Same shipment: MY-DHL-20265102
Customer: Zulaikha Hassan

Customer email: Your delivery man (the one who came to Bukit Jalil area) was extremely rude to me. I asked him nicely to help me carry the box and he just threw it at the door and left. I want a formal apology.

Customer WhatsApp message complains courier collected RM450 COD instead of
RM250 shown on invoice. Customer attached photo of handwritten receipt.
Email from finance later says system reflects RM450 COD amount.
Warehouse note: “COD sticker unclear, handwritten?”
Caller wants refund of extra RM200 urgently. No clarity on who entered wrong
COD amount.

Incoming email from customer on 22 March with subject: “Damaged parcel received – unacceptable condition”. 



Customer attaches 4 images showing a crushed DHL box, torn side panels, and bubble wrap exposed. Product inside appears to be a coffee machine (customer did not confirm brand).



Email body states shipment MY987623400 arrived two days late and external box was already damaged when handed over. Customer did not reject delivery because courier mentioned “cannot take back once scanned”. After opening the box, customer noticed water leakage marks and dents on the machine casing.

Internal Teams chat between delivery partner and hub supervisor mentions: “Package already dented when offloaded from linehaul, maybe stacking issue.” Another message says, “No exception scanned at hub, driver might have forgotten.”

Call center notes say customer demanded replacement or refund and is threatening to post photos on social media. Agent marked case as “damage – investigation needed” but forgot to tag the insurance/claims team.

Handwritten warehouse remark uploaded later reads: “Inbound pallet unstable, one carton collapsed, taped back.” No pallet ID referenced.

Issue involves late delivery, damaged item, missing exception scan, and complaint escalation. Multiple teams involved but no clear timeline or responsible department. Priority unclear.

Email from: procurement@medisupply.com.my

Subject: Non-arrival of critical medical shipment



Dear DHL Support,



We wish to inform you that shipment MY-DHL-20269872 containing an Automated External Defibrillator (AED) unit and surgical consumables valued at RM15,800 has not been delivered as of today, 13 May 2026. The agreed delivery window was 10 May 2026.



This equipment is required for a scheduled surgical procedure at KPJ Damansara Specialist Hospital on 15 May 2026. Failure to deliver by 14 May 2026 will result in procedure cancellation affecting patient welfare.



Tracking shows last scan on 9 May at Shah Alam hub. No further updates since.



We request immediate escalation and status update.



Regards,

Ahmad Faris

Procurement Manager

MediSupply Sdn Bhd

Customer WhatsApp message complains courier collected RM450 COD instead of RM250 shown on invoice. Customer attached photo of handwritten receipt.
Email from finance later says system reflects RM450 COD amount.
Warehouse note: “COD sticker unclear, handwritten?”
Caller wants refund of extra RM200 urgently. No clarity on who entered wrong COD amount.

From: Ahmad Fariz bin Zulkifli 
Date: May 12, 2026 
Channel: Customer Email 
 
Subject: Missing Parcel - Tracking MY78901234 - 7 Days Overdue 
 
Dear DHL Customer Support, 
 
I am writing to report that my parcel with tracking number MY78901234  
has not been delivered and I have not received it as of today, May 12,  
2026. The expected delivery date was May 5, 2026 — it is now 7 days  
overdue with no update. 
 
I ordered electronic equipment (a laptop) valued at RM1,200 from an  
online retailer. The retailer confirmed the item was handed to DHL on  
May 3, 2026 from Kuala Lumpur. 
 
When I check the tracking portal, the last scan shows the parcel  
departed Shah Alam warehouse on May 7, 2026 at 08:45AM. After that  
there are no further scans. The parcel seems to have disappeared after  
leaving Shah Alam. I cannot find any update after that date. 
 
I have contacted your hotline twice — on May 9 and May 11 — but both  
times I was told to wait. I cannot wait any longer as this is an  
expensive item and I suspect it may have been lost or stolen at the  
facility. 
 
I am requesting an immediate investigation. If the parcel cannot be  
located within 48 hours I will be filing a formal complaint and seeking  
compensation for the full value of RM1,200. I also reserve the right to  
escalate this to the Consumer Claims Tribunal if not resolved urgently. 
 
Regards, 
Ahmad Fariz bin Zulkifli 
Contact: 012-3456789

Customer email reports parcel (tracking: MY55678901) not delivered after 5 days despite standard 3-day service. Customer states they were home all day on the expected delivery date but no courier arrived and no delivery attempt notification was received. A neighbour mentioned seeing a DHL van in the area that morning.
Driver log shows delivery attempted at 10:42 AM but marked as "customer unavailable." Customer disputes this and requests immediate redelivery or collection from nearest DHL service point by end of week.

Customer email reports a parcel (tracking: MY55678901) addressed to 12 Jalan Kenanga, Subang Jaya was delivered to 12 Jalan Kenanga, Shah Alam instead. Customer confirmed the parcel was signed for by an unknown person at the wrong address. Customer is requesting the parcel to be retrieved and redelivered to the correct address.
